linkedin_drafts.title: Guardrails, not prompts
linkedin_drafts.body: Most agent failures are not model failures. They are operating failures: no scope, no proof rule, no delivery lock, and no one clear moment where work counts as done. AtlasOps Agent Skill Packs package the missing operating layer: SKILL.md files, AGENTS.md files, checks, blockers, QA contracts, and delivery policies.

linkedin_drafts.title: Codex repo control
linkedin_drafts.body: If your coding agent keeps changing too many files, the fix is not another prompt. It is a repo control guardrail: ownership boundaries, test commands, dirty-worktree rules, and evidence before completion.

linkedin_drafts.title: Stripe payment proof
linkedin_drafts.body: A checkout URL is not payment proof. A success redirect is not payment proof. For downloadable products, delivery should wait for signed Stripe evidence with matching order metadata.

linkedin_drafts.title: Social publishing proof
linkedin_drafts.body: Opening a social composer is not a post. Typing text into a composer is not a post. Public content automation needs permalink proof, source discipline, and a rule against debug-looking labels.

linkedin_drafts.title: RAG source discipline
linkedin_drafts.body: A RAG workflow should not sound confident just because it retrieved something. The useful layer is a source packet: current sources, validation notes, conflict handling, and a clear gate for unsupported claims.

linkedin_drafts.title: OpenClaw + Codex safely
linkedin_drafts.body: Tool bridges need doctor checks, gateway proof, version awareness, rollback plans, and repo boundaries. That is the difference between an integration and a reliable operating path.

linkedin_drafts.title: Agent Company Launch Kit
linkedin_drafts.body: When agents start acting like a team, they need company-shaped controls: owners, tickets, routines, budgets, QA review, and proof requirements. Agent speed needs an operating system.

linkedin_drafts.title: Revenue agents
linkedin_drafts.body: Revenue automation should be strict about what counts. Leads are not sales. Checkout sessions are not revenue. Test payments are not live revenue. Signed payment evidence is where the state changes.

linkedin_drafts.title: AI SEO guardrails
linkedin_drafts.body: AI visibility work needs more than new content. It needs entity clarity, service-page proof, FAQ structure, and checks that prevent unsupported visibility claims.

linkedin_drafts.title: Setup review
linkedin_drafts.body: A Guardrail Setup Review is for the buyer who already knows the workflow is important and wants Atlas to check the install path, evidence rules, and failure modes before agents use it.

x_drafts: Guardrails, not prompts. Agent Skill Packs package checks, blockers, evidence rules, SKILL.md files, AGENTS.md files, and QA contracts for real workflows.
A checkout link is not payment proof. A success redirect is not payment proof. Delivery should wait for signed Stripe evidence.
If Codex keeps touching too many files, add repo control: scope, ownership, tests, dirty-worktree rules, and proof before done.
Social automation needs permalink proof. Browser-open and composer-open are not posts.
RAG agents need source packets: current sources, validation notes, uncertainty, and conflict handling.
OpenClaw + Codex needs doctor checks, gateway status, rollback plans, and repo boundaries before tool actions count.
Agent companies need tickets, owners, routines, budgets, QA, and proof. Heartbeats are not work.
Revenue agents should not count checkout sessions as revenue. Signed payment evidence changes state.
Agent Skill Bundles group the controls buyers usually need together: builder, social, revenue, OpenClaw + Codex, and company launch.
The useful question is not 'what prompt should I use?' It is 'what evidence proves this workflow is done?'

field_note_outlines: Why agent teams need operating controls before more autonomy
The difference between a checkout session and payment evidence
How to turn recurring agent failures into reusable Agent Skill Packs

buyer_objection_responses.objection: Is this just a prompt pack?
buyer_objection_responses.response: No. The packs include operational rules, evidence requirements, agent instruction files, QA contracts, and delivery policies.

buyer_objection_responses.objection: Can I buy one pack instead of a bundle?
buyer_objection_responses.response: Yes. Each $99 Agent Skill Pack is sold separately. Bundles are for workflows that need multiple controls together.

buyer_objection_responses.objection: Does checkout mean I get an instant download?
buyer_objection_responses.response: No. Download delivery unlocks only after signed Stripe payment evidence matches the order.
Yes. The Guardrail Setup Review checks the fit, install path, evidence rules, and acceptance criteria.

buyer_objection_responses.objection: Do you publish social posts automatically?
buyer_objection_responses.response: No. Drafts can be prepared, but publishing requires proof from the platform and current social gates.
